verses.commentary: After Sabbath, toward dawn on the first day of the week (mia ton sabbaton, μία τῶν σαββάτων) — Sunday. Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb. The women resume the watch.
Great earthquake (seismos megas, σεισμὸς μέγας — same word as 27:51). Angel of the Lord descends, rolls back the stone, sits on it. The stone-rolling is not to let Jesus out but to let witnesses in.
Appearance like lightning, clothes white as snow. Theophany imagery (Daniel 7:9; 10:6). Heaven's messenger at the heart of earthly defeat.
Guards trembled, became like dead men. The dead-pretenders are stricken; the truly-dead one rises. Reversal.
Angel to the women: 'Do not fear. I know you seek Jesus the crucified.' The cross is the identification — he is not severed from his suffering identity even in resurrection.
'He is not here, for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where he lay.' Empty tomb evidence. Resurrection authenticated by absence.
'Go quickly, tell his disciples he has risen and is going before you to Galilee — there you will see him.' Commission to the women — they are the first apostles to the apostles.
They depart quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy. Run to tell the disciples. Mixed affect — terror does not erase joy; joy does not erase awe.
Jesus meets them: 'Greetings!' (chairete, χαίρετε). They grasp his feet and worship him. The women's worship is recorded as proper response.
'Do not fear. Go and tell my brothers (adelphois, ἀδελφοῖς) to go to Galilee — there they will see me.' Family language for the failed disciples. Restoration before reproof.
Some of the guard reported to the chief priests all that had happened. The fact of the resurrection is reported by the very ones posted to prevent it.
Chief priests met with elders, took counsel, gave large sum of money to the soldiers. Bribery of witnesses. The same machinery that bought Judas now buys the cover-up.
Instructions: say 'His disciples came by night and stole him while we slept.' A sleeping witness who testifies to what happened while sleeping is self-refuting.
'If this comes to the governor's ears, we will persuade him and keep you out of trouble.' Promise of cover-up. The conspiracy thickens.
They took the money, did as instructed. 'And this story is spread among the Jews to this day' — Matthew explains a contemporary counter-narrative he is rebutting.
The eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain Jesus had appointed. Mountain — the place of teaching, transfiguration, sermon. Final commissioning happens where teaching happened.

When they saw him, they worshiped (prosekynesan, προσεκύνησαν); but some doubted (edistasan, ἐδίστασαν — split-mind, same word as Peter on water 14:31). Honest record: worship and doubt coexisted in the resurrection witnesses.

Jesus comes, speaks: 'All authority (pasa exousia, πᾶσα ἐξουσία) in heaven and on earth has been given to me.' The risen Lord receives cosmic authority. Daniel 7:14 fulfilled — the Son of Man given dominion over all peoples.

'Go therefore (poreuthentes, πορευθέντες) and make disciples of all nations (panta ta ethne, πάντα τὰ ἔθνη), baptizing them in the name (singular: onoma) of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit.' Trinitarian baptismal formula. The earlier restriction (10:5) is now reversed: all nations included. The 'name' is singular — one God in three persons.

'Teaching them to observe all that I have commanded — and behold, I am with you always (pasas tas hemeras, πάσας τὰς ἡμέρας, all the days), to the end of the age (synteleia tou aionos, συντέλεια τοῦ αἰῶνος).' The Gospel that began with 'Immanuel — God with us' (1:23) closes with 'I am with you.' The promise is fulfilled and extended into the church age.
